verdict_reasoning: KLK2 is canonically a secreted serine protease (no TM domain, no GPI anchor). However, recent peer-reviewed studies and clinical data confirm cell surface localization in prostate cancer cells (FACS, IHC, multiplex immunofluorescence on VCaP cells and patient-derived mCRPC tumors). Surface expression is tissue-restricted (prostate epithelial/cancer cells) and low-level in most preclinical models. Multiple surface-directed therapeutics are in active clinical development: pasritamig (KLK2×CD3 bispecific T-cell engager, Phase 3), KLK2 CAR-T, and 225Ac-KLK2 radioligand — all validated against cell surface KLK2. The anchoring mechanism is unresolved but the surface pool is functionally established, making contextual the appropriate call.
